title: Keep metric label cardinality bounded
description: Restrict metric labels to low-cardinality dimensions and push high-cardinality identifiers to logs or traces to avoid blowing up the metrics backend.
body: Use when adding labels/tags to a metric. Do not attach user ids, request ids, full URLs, or unbounded values as metric labels, and avoid free-form strings in dimensions. Actions: enumerate the label's possible values, keep dimensions bounded (status class, route template, region), and route per-request detail to structured logs or trace attributes instead. Record the cardinality estimate per metric. Validation signals include a series-count check staying within budget and an alert if cardinality spikes. Risk boundary: an unbounded label can exhaust the metrics store and cause an observability outage during an incident — escalate before adding high-cardinality dimensions.
